channel.title: NBC Sports Fantasy Football News
channel.description: Latest player news and analysis for fantasy football from NBC Sports.

item.title: Panthers agreed to terms with WR John Metchie, formerly of the Jets, on a one-year contract.
item.description: Metchie was traded to New York from Philadelphia midseason. He was more productive as a Jet, but fellow trade acquisition Adonai Mitchell quickly blew by him in the pecking order for targets. Metchie finished the season with 33 catches for 274 yards and two scores, nearly all of which came in New York. He will be fighting for a rotational role in Carolina this offseason.

item.title: Browns did not tender restricted free agent TE Blake Whiteheart, making him a free agent.
item.description: They also non-tendered Mohamoud Diabate, Sam Kamara, and Sam Webb. Whiteheart had a brief flash of fantasy relevance last year and, entering his age-26 season, might pop up elsewhere in 2026.

item.title: Bills agreed to terms with LB Bradley Chubb, formerly of the Dolphins, on a three-year, $43.5 million contract.
item.description: The deal includes a whopping $29 million in guaranteed money and ties the former Pro Bowl linebacker to the Bills through the 2028 season. Chubb managed a solid 8.5 sacks in 2025, his first year after a torn ACL that cost the seven-year veteran the entirety of 2024. Injuries have been an issue throughout Chubb’s career, missing 17 games in his first four season with the Broncos before the 17 missed games two season ago with the Dolphins. The Bills now invest significant money after a fully healthy season last year. He’ll join a stacked second level in Buffalo as he looks to regain his Pro Bowl form.

item.title: Dolphins re-signed CB A.J. Green.
item.description: Green signed with the Browns as a UDFA in 2020 and carved out a large role on special teams in his second season. He also got some run at quarterback before joining the Rams last year. He was cut by LA and bounced between their active roster and practice squad for half the season. The Dolphins eventually claimed him and are now giving him a shot to make the roster in 2026.

item.title: Broncos agreed to terms with TE Lucas Krull.
item.description: Krull must still pass a physical. The 27-year-old tight end suffered a Jones fracture last season and was placed on injured reserve in early October. He resumed practicing in January, but did not resume playing. In three games last season, he caught 2-of-4 targets for 15 yards. He is not relevant in fantasy.

item.title: Bears agreed to terms with OT Jedrick Willis Jr., formerly of the Browns, on a one-year contract.
item.description: It’s a prove-it deal for Wills, who missed all of 2025 while recovering from knee issues that date all the way back to 2023. The Bears re-signed left tackle Braxton Jones this week, but Willis could at least give him a run for the starting job in camp. More likely is that Wills will serve as a swing tackle for Chicago this year.

item.title: Dolphins signed CB Darrell Baker, formerly of the Titans, to a one-year contract.
item.description: Baker spent the last two seasons with the Titans. Although he led the Titans’ secondary with eight pass breakups last year, he also allowed the most receptions (42), receiving yards (621) and receiving touchdowns (five). Baker will likely compete for a starting role in Miami.

item.title: Titans agreed to terms with EDGE Malik Herring, formerly of the Chiefs, on a one-year contract.
item.description: The financials of the deal were not immediately made available but it appears to be of the “prove it” nature. Herring, a former undrafted free agent signee with the Chiefs, developed into a solid role player and provides much needed depth off the edge for the Titans.

item.title: Texans released DL Mario Edwards Jr.
item.description: It was with a failed physical designation. Edwards tore his pectoral muscle in December and isn’t fully recovered yet. It’s possible he’ll be an option elsewhere in 2026, but considering the Texans knew the medicals better than anyone, it could be a long recovery.

item.title: Packers agreed to terms with DT Javon Hargrave, formerly of the Vikings, on a two-year, $23 million contract.
item.description: The deal includes $13 million in 2026, potentially giving Green Bay an early out if the 10-year veteran starts showing signs of aging following his age-33 season. The former two-time Pro Bowler played the 2025 season with the Vikings, registering only 3.5 sacks and 52 tackles in 16 appearances. He did prove healthy last year after playing only three games for the 49ers in 2024 and brings another capable body up front for the retooled Packers.
